reasoning: As of late October 2025, Elon Musk is a significant longshot to win TIME's Person of the Year for 2025. At the start of the year he was a betting favorite, but the tragic death of Pope Francis and subsequent election of Robert Prevost as Pope Leo XIV (the first American Pope) in May 2025 created a massive new frontrunner. In addition, Artificial Intelligence continues to dominate prediction market shares (often cited around 25-35%), and Donald Trump remains a prominent candidate. Across prediction market dashboards like Kalshi and various sportsbooks in August and September 2025, Musk's implied probability has fallen to approximately 4%. Since TIME rarely awards the title multiple times to the same individual in such a short span unless they are a sitting US president, Musk's 2021 win further depresses his base rate. The combination of fierce competition from historically unprecedented events (an American Pope, the AI explosion) and Musk's low market odds warrants a minimum allowable estimate of 5%.
